_about: Classification of upstream test corpora that drive example/real-world fixtures: testdata/examples.md (examples_test.go), testdata/crowdsec.json (test/crowdsec), testdata/coredns (test/coredns), testdata/generated.txt (test/gen fuzz output), testdata/crash.txt (fuzz crash regression).

items.name: TestExamples
items.reason: Markdown-embedded expressions over a fixed env (let/map/groupBy/split/toPairs pipelines). All language features used are ported and covered by the expr/builtin corpora. Running the .md directly needs a markdown-fence extractor adapter + the example env; the underlying language behavior is PASS.

items.name: TestCrowdsec
items.reason: Real-world security-rule corpus bound to a Go struct env (crowdsec funcs.go custom functions + structs). Requires porting the crowdsec env/funcs as a JS adapter. Expression surface is supported; not yet adapted.

items.name: TestCoreDNS
items.reason: Real-world DNS-policy corpus bound to a Go struct env. Same adapter requirement as crowdsec.

items.name: TestGenerated
items.reason: Output of Go's native fuzzer (test/gen). The generated expressions reference Go-typed env fields (fixed-width ints, pointers, Stringer); replaying them requires the Go fuzz env shape. Fuzzing itself is Go-native (FORCED_DIVERGENCE).

items.name: FuzzCrashRegression
items.reason: A single Go fuzz-crash regression seed for go test -run=FuzzExpr; Go-native fuzzing artifact, no JS analog.

note: The crowdsec/coredns/examples corpora are additive PASS_WITH_ADAPTER candidates (env-adapter work), not behavioral gaps: every language feature they exercise is already verified by the expr (139) and builtin (137) corpora.
